examples.title: Basic Function Template - Swap
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental function template syntax for swapping two values
examples.explanation: The template<typename T> declares T as a type parameter. The compiler generates two separate functions: swap(int&, int&) and swap(double&, double&) based on usage. This demonstrates compile-time polymorphism.

examples.title: Function Template with Multiple Type Parameters
examples.description: Template working with multiple different types
examples.explanation: Multiple type parameters allow the function to work with different input types. The return type uses both template parameters to create a pair of heterogeneous types.

examples.title: Function Template Specialization
examples.description: Full specialization for custom behavior with specific types
examples.explanation: The template<> syntax indicates full specialization. For const char*, it uses strcmp for proper string comparison instead of pointer address comparison. When specialization matches, it's preferred over the generic template.

examples.title: Template with Default Arguments
examples.description: Function template with default template parameters
examples.explanation: Default template arguments provide fallback types when not specified. This makes templates more flexible and convenient for common use cases.

examples.title: Non-Type Template Parameters
examples.description: Using non-type parameters in function templates
examples.explanation: Non-type template parameters accept compile-time constant values. They're useful for creating parameterized types, array sizes, and compile-time computations.

examples.title: Explicit Template Argument Specification
examples.explanation: Explicit template arguments are specified in angle brackets after the function name. They override automatic deduction, useful when deduction fails or when you want a specific type.

examples.title: SFINAE Example with enable_if
examples.description: Using SFINAE to enable/disable functions based on type traits
examples.explanation: SFINAE enables template functions conditionally based on type properties. std::enable_if_t creates a substitution failure for incompatible types, causing the compiler to try other overloads.
